title: The Lantern of Bell Hollow
covenant: A fishing hollow where the harbor bell rings itself the night before a storm, and the lighthouse keeper has not been seen since the last ringing. The boats want bringing home, and the lantern wants a hand that remembers the old courtesy.
styleBible: Romantic dark-fantasy oil painting with gold-leaf light, wet stone and lamplit fog, expressive faces, and restrained PG-13 peril.

hero.name: Wren
hero.bearing: A sea-gray coat two sizes too honest, a concertina slung like a shield, and boots that have argued with every pier in the Hollow.
hero.background: A harbor singer who learned early that the right verse can hold a door open or a storm shut.
hero.mark: a white streak in dark hair, shaped like a gull's wing

narration_blocks.text: Bell Hollow wakes to a bell nobody rang. The harbor fog stands in the lanes like a patient customer, and every boat at the moorings faces the sea as if it had been asked to. Wren knows the courtesy: when the bell rings itself, somebody climbs to the light.

narration_blocks.speaker: Edlyn
narration_blocks.text: The keeper's window has been dark three nights. Take the cliff stair, singer — and take your manners. The lamp was lit by a promise older than the pier.

state_updates.chronicle_add: The harbor bell rang itself, and Wren was asked to climb to the dark lighthouse.

arc.title: The Lantern of Bell Hollow
arc.evil_plot: The Tidewife is unpicking the old promise that keeps the light lit, so the boats will follow her bell instead of the harbor's.
arc.stakes: Every boat on the water, the keeper's life, and the promise that holds the lamp.
arc.style_bible: Romantic dark-fantasy oil painting with gold-leaf light, wet stone and lamplit fog, expressive faces, and restrained PG-13 peril.

cast_add.name: Edlyn
cast_add.visual: A broad, weatherproof woman in an oilskin the color of old kelp, iron-gray braid pinned with a fishhook, and a ledger under one arm that has outlived four piers.

cast_add.name: The Tidewife
cast_add.visual: A tall figure of harbor water given a gown's shape, hung with net-lace and drowned lanterns, her face a calm low tide that never quite settles.

turns.player: I climb the cliff stair to the lighthouse and call out for the keeper.

narration_blocks.text: The stair takes her breath and pays back the view. At the top, the great lamp room stands cold, but the storm-glass door is unlatched — left open by someone who meant to come back. Wren sets her palm to the brass and the wick takes flame from nothing but the courtesy of being asked. The lantern answers the dark with a steady gold.

state_updates.chronicle_add: Wren relit the great lamp with the old courtesy; the keeper's door stood unlatched.

turns.player: I search the lamp room for any sign of where the keeper went.

narration_blocks.text: The lamp room keeps a tidy grief: a chair still warm-shaped, a mug gone cold, and on the map table a chart of the Hollow weighted open with a whelk shell. A wet handprint glistens on the sea-side glass, five fingers and no arm to own them, pointing the way the boats have been facing all morning.

suggestions: Follow the handprint's line on the chart

state_updates.chronicle_add: A wet handprint on the lamp-room glass pointed seaward, toward the Teeth.

turns.player: I take the chart down to Edlyn and ask what stands out where the handprint pointed.

narration_blocks.speaker: Edlyn
narration_blocks.text: That line runs to the Teeth — the drowned stacks past the breakwater. Nothing honest anchors there.

narration_blocks.speaker: Brother Halm
narration_blocks.text: Forgive the interruption. I keep the chapel ledger, and the ledger keeps a debt: once a generation, the light asks to be carried out to the Teeth and shown to the water, so the sea remembers whose harbor this is. The keeper went to pay it early. Alone.

suggestions: Ask Halm why the keeper went early

state_updates.chronicle_add: Brother Halm named the old debt: the light must be shown to the water at the Teeth, once a generation.

cast_add.name: Brother Halm
cast_add.visual: A small round friar in salt-stained wool, spectacles fogged from habit, carrying a chapel ledger chained to his belt like an anchor he trusts.

turns.player: I press Halm for what the ledger really says about who drew the harbor's first chart.

narration_blocks.speaker: Brother Halm
narration_blocks.text: The first chart is signed only 'the cartographer,' but the ledger's margin keeps what the town agreed to forget. The cartographer's true name is Ilvane Moor. She sounded the Teeth, sold the soundings twice, and the second buyer was the sea.

state_updates.chronicle_add: The chapel ledger yielded a name the town had agreed to forget.

turns.player: I borrow Edlyn's dory and row for the Teeth with the storm-lantern lashed to the bow.

narration_blocks.text: The Hollow lets her go the way a hand lets go a coin it means to get back. Past the breakwater the fog opens a lane exactly one dory wide, and the water under the hull goes quiet as held breath. Out on the Teeth, a second light answers hers — smaller, guttering, held by someone sitting very still.

suggestions: Hail the light on the Teeth

state_updates.chronicle_add: Wren rowed the fog lane to the Teeth, where a small light answered the lantern.

turns.player: I sing the harbor courtesy across the water as I bring the dory alongside the rocks.

narration_blocks.text: Her verse crosses the water and the water lets it. On the lowest stack sits the keeper, gray to the lips, one boot wedged in a tide-crack, holding his little lamp toward the sea like a man paying at a counter that never opens. Below the surface, net-lace and drowned lanterns turn a slow circle: the Tidewife, listening to the song with her whole cold patience.

narration_blocks.speaker: The Keeper
narration_blocks.text: She wants the debt paid to HER bell. Don't let the big light look away from the water, singer. It's the looking that pays.

suggestions: Hold the lantern's beam on the water
Offer the Tidewife a verse instead

state_updates.chronicle_add: Wren found the keeper trapped on the Teeth, the Tidewife circling beneath, the debt still unpaid.

turns.player: I hold the lantern's beam steady on the water and finish the courtesy, every verse of it.

narration_blocks.text: The beam lies gold on the tide and Wren pays the debt in full: every verse, the old names in the old order, while the keeper's small lamp adds its trembling interest. The circling below slows, then stills. The Tidewife rises just enough to be a face, and the face is — satisfied. The sea remembers whose harbor this is, and lets go of the boot it was holding.

narration_blocks.speaker: Edlyn
narration_blocks.text: Bell rang itself again at dawn — once, polite, like a customer leaving a tip. Get some sleep, singer. The Hollow keeps what you did in its ledger now.

state_updates.chronicle_add: The debt was paid in gold light and old verses; the sea released the keeper, and the bell rang once in thanks.
